# FTD2XX

{{ package }} {{ lint }}

{{ python }} {{ black }}

ftd2xx is a simple python wrapper around the [D2XX DLL] from FTDI using
ctypes. The API based on Pablo Bleyer Kocik's [d2xx] extension.

Requires Python 3.8 minimum. Please ensure you have FTDI drivers installed or
available where the linker looks for shared libraries (e.g., PATH on windows,
LD_LIBRARY_PATH or standard library directories on Linux)

I don't have time to maintain this project, so I am looking for a maintainer.

There is another library by [pyftdi] that looks more actively maintained, has support for newer device, and may fit your needs better.

Also, Jeremy Bentham wrote a [blog] about using this library.

[blog]: https://iosoft.blog/2018/12/02/ftdi-python-part-1/
[d2xx]: http://bleyer.org/pyusb/
[d2xx dll]: http://www.ftdichip.com/Drivers/D2XX.htm
[pyftdi]: https://github.com/eblot/pyftdi

classes.name: ftd2xx.defines.OpenExFlags
documentation: Used to indicate the type of identifier being passed to FT_OpenEx.

modules.name: ftd2xx
documentation:
Control FTDI USB chips.

Use {any}`ftd2xx.open` or {any}`ftd2xx.openEx`.
\:example:

> with open(0) as device:
>
> : device.write(b"Hello World!")

documentation: don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: ftd2xx._ftd2xx_linux
documentation: from ctypes import CDLL, c_char_p, c_char,

modules.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx
documentation:
Module for accessing functions from FTD2XX in an easier to use
_pythonic_ way. For full documentation please refer to the FTDI
Programming Guide. This module is based on Pablo Bleyers d2xx module,
except this uses ctypes instead of an extension approach.

functions.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.call_ft
documentation: Call an FTDI function and check the status. Raise exception on error

functions.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.listDevices
documentation:
Return a list of serial numbers(default), descriptions or
locations (Windows only) of the connected FTDI devices depending on value
of flags

functions.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.getLibraryVersion
documentation: Return a long representing library version

functions.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.createDeviceInfoList
documentation: Create the internal device info list and return number of entries

functions.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.getDeviceInfoDetail
documentation:
Get an entry from the internal device info list. Set update to
False to avoid a slow call to createDeviceInfoList.

functions.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.open
documentation:
Open a handle to a usb device by index and return an FTD2XX instance for
it. Set update to False to avoid a slow call to createDeviceInfoList.

Args:
    dev (int): The device number to open.
    update (bool): Set to False to disable automatic call to createDeviceInfoList.

Raises:
    DeviceError: If the device cannot be opened.

Returns:
    An instance of the FTD2XX class if successful. Use it as a context manager.

Example:
    with open(0, False) as dev:
        dev.write(b"Hello World")

functions.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.openEx
documentation:
Open a handle to a usb device by serial number(default), description or
location(Windows only) depending on value of flags and return an FTD2XX
instance for it. Set update to False to avoid a slow call to createDeviceInfoList.

Args:
    id_str (bytes): The ID string from listDevices.
    flags (int): Flag (consult D2XX Guide). Defaults to OPEN_BY_SERIAL_NUMBER.
    update (bool): Set to False to disable automatic call to createDeviceInfoList.

Raises:
    DeviceError: If the device cannot be opened.

Returns:
    An instance of the FTD2XX class if successful. Use it as a context manager.

Example:
    with openEx(b"MyDevice", update=False) as dev:
        dev.write(b"Hello World")

functions.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.getVIDPID
documentation: Linux only. Get the VID and PID of the device

functions.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.setVIDPID
documentation: Linux only. Set the VID and PID of the device

classes.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.__init__
documentation:
Create an instance of the FTD2XX class with the given device handle
and populate the device info in the instance dictionary.

Args:
    update (bool): Set False to disable automatic (slow) call to
                    createDeviceInfoList

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.read
documentation:
Read up to nchars bytes of data from the device. Can return fewer if
timedout. Use getQueueStatus to find how many bytes are available

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.write
documentation:
Send the data to the device. Data must be a string representing the
bytes to be sent

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.setDivisor
documentation: Set the clock divider. The clock will be set to 6e6/(div + 1).

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.setDataCharacteristics
documentation: Set the data characteristics for UART

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.setFlowControl
documentation: Set the flow control for UART

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.setDtr
documentation: Set the DTR (Data Terminal Ready) signal of the FTDI device.

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.clrDtr
documentation: Clear the DTR signal of the FTDI device.

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.setRts
documentation: Set the RTS (Request To Send) signal of the FTDI device.

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.clrRts
documentation: Clear the RTS signal of the FTDI device.

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.getModemStatus
documentation: Get the modem status of the FTDI device.

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.setChars
documentation: Set the event and error characters for UART

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.purge
documentation: Purge the receive and/or transmit buffers

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.setTimeouts
documentation: Set the read and write timeouts in milliseconds

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.setDeadmanTimeout
documentation: Set the deadman timeout in milliseconds

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.getQueueStatus
documentation: Get number of bytes in receive queue.

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.getStatus
documentation:
Return a 3-tuple of rx queue bytes, tx queue bytes and event
status

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.setUSBParameters
documentation: Set the USB request transfer sizes

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.getDeviceInfo
documentation: Returns a dictionary describing the device.

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.getComPortNumber
documentation: Return a long representing the COM port number

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.eeProgram
documentation:
Program the EEPROM with custom data. If SerialNumber is null, a new
serial number is generated from ManufacturerId

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.eeRead
documentation: Get the program information from the EEPROM

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.eeUASize
documentation: Get the EEPROM user area size

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.eeUAWrite
documentation:
Write data to the EEPROM user area. data must be a bytes object with
appropriate byte values

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.eeUARead
documentation: Read b_to_read bytes from the EEPROM user area

methods.name: ftd2xx.ftd2xx.FTD2XX.__exit__
documentation: Close the device when exiting the context manager

Class for communicating with an FTDI device

Use {any}`open` or {any}`openEx` to create an instance of this class.
